skos_definition: Set is a mathematical concept used as an object, quantity, structure, or named idea in mathematical discourse.
skos_example: Use set as the spoken semantic name for this concept in accessible math output.
provenance_note: OntoMathPRO class mathematics:E847 has direct rdfs:label Set@en; selected as canonical IRI according to Week 2 rules
accessibility_note: Speech rendering should use the canonical label and a concise definition when the notation may be ambiguous.

skos_definition: Subset is a mathematical relation used to compare or connect mathematical objects.
skos_example: Use subset when explaining how two mathematical objects or expressions are connected.
provenance_note: OpenMath Official set1 Content Dictionary includes subset as a relation symbol; selected as canonical source because no safer OntoMathPRO exact class was confirmed
accessibility_note: Speech rendering should make the relation explicit rather than relying only on symbol names.
